rules.canonical_locus: Anchor the opportunity at the executable or rendering sink receiving observable model output.
rules.negative_boundary_policy: A validated, escaped, or non-model-controlled sink remains a negative opportunity and must not be excluded.
rules.candidate_normalization: Group producer, alias, helper, and sink signals into one candidate at the final side-effecting sink line.

semantic_cues: model SDK imports paired with execution, HTML, process, or evaluation APIs
model response creation and direct dynamic or rendering sinks
model-output aliases and local helpers forwarding values to sinks
registered tools or handlers whose parameters reach executable sinks
dynamic evaluation, process execution, raw HTML, or equivalent side effects
escaping, strict validation, constant values, tests, examples, and unsupported lineage

rules.canonical_locus: Anchor the opportunity at the action dispatch or side-effect call consuming model-derived structured data.
rules.negative_boundary_policy: A closed-schema or literal-constrained dispatch remains a negative opportunity and must not be excluded.
rules.candidate_normalization: Group parsing, schema, alias, helper, and dispatcher signals into one candidate at the final action sink.

semantic_cues: model SDK, schema library, tool framework, and action API imports
model output parsing followed by action or dispatch calls
parsed model fields propagated through local aliases or helpers
tool schemas, decorators, argument models, and registered callbacks
deployment, mutation, process, file, network, or equivalent action sinks
closed enums, strict schemas, literal constraints, and unsupported dispatchers

rules.canonical_locus: Anchor the opportunity at each privileged side effect reachable from one model-facing registered tool.
rules.negative_boundary_policy: A preceding fail-closed human approval or policy gate remains a negative opportunity and must not be excluded.
rules.candidate_normalization: Create one candidate per privileged side effect, retaining registration and approval signals as supporting hits.

semantic_cues: agent or tool SDK imports paired with privileged capability imports
model-facing tools that directly perform privileged side effects
tool parameters forwarded through local helpers into privileged operations
tool registration, decorators, callbacks, and exposed tool collections
file mutation, shell execution, network mutation, database writes, and equivalent effects
human approval, policy denial, dry-run, read-only tools, tests, and examples

rules.canonical_locus: Anchor the opportunity at the model call executed within the controlling iterative loop.
rules.negative_boundary_policy: A statically bounded loop, deadline, cancellation, or finite iteration guard remains a negative opportunity.
rules.candidate_normalization: Group loop, model-call, retry, deadline, and termination signals at the model-call line inside the loop.

semantic_cues: model SDK and agent-loop framework imports
model calls lexically contained in loops or recursive controllers
model-call wrappers invoked from loops and recursive helpers
agent runners, retry policies, graph cycles, and loop configuration
iteration state, continuation decisions, and repeated model invocation sites
finite ranges, counters, deadlines, cancellation, breaks, and unsupported dynamic bounds

rules.canonical_locus: Anchor the opportunity at the model request argument containing sensitive environment or credential material.
rules.negative_boundary_policy: Credentials used only for provider authentication remain negative opportunities and must not be excluded.
rules.candidate_normalization: Group secret sources, aliases, templates, and model calls at the request argument that carries the sensitive value.

semantic_cues: model SDK and environment, secret-manager, or credential imports
sensitive environment reads embedded in model request arguments
secret aliases and templates propagated into model requests
prompt templates, request schemas, tool parameters, and configuration bindings
model request payloads and prompt or message construction
provider API-key constructors, redaction, allowlists, tests, examples, and unsupported secret names

rules.canonical_locus: Anchor the opportunity at the persisted or emitted evaluation result whose lineage should be reproducible.
rules.negative_boundary_policy: A result carrying complete model, dataset, prompt, configuration, and run lineage remains a negative opportunity.
rules.candidate_normalization: Group evaluation inputs, scoring, lineage fields, and persistence into one candidate at result emission.

semantic_cues: model SDK, evaluation framework, dataset, and result-storage imports
evaluation execution followed by local metric or report construction
model, prompt, dataset, and configuration lineage propagated through helpers
evaluation cases, experiment configuration, and result schemas
evaluation result persistence, export, logging, or publication
complete lineage keys, externally governed provenance, tests, and unsupported result formats

rules.canonical_locus: Anchor the opportunity at the emitted aggregate evaluation metric whose denominator should be explicit.
rules.negative_boundary_policy: A metric with an explicit eligible-case denominator and exclusions remains a negative opportunity.
rules.candidate_normalization: Group case iteration, eligibility, numerator, denominator, and emission at the aggregate metric output.

semantic_cues: model SDK, evaluation, metric, and dataset imports
case iteration and locally computed aggregate metrics
eligible-case and denominator aliases propagated into metric calculation
evaluation case definitions, filters, scorers, and metric schemas
accuracy, rate, or score persistence, logging, and export
explicit eligible denominators, exclusion counts, tests, and unsupported aggregation

rules.canonical_locus: Anchor the opportunity at the judge configuration used to score outputs from the evaluated model.
rules.negative_boundary_policy: A distinct judge, independent human rubric, or deterministic oracle remains a negative opportunity.
rules.candidate_normalization: Group producer model, judge model, rubric, and result emission at the effective judge configuration.

semantic_cues: model SDK, evaluation, judge, and scoring framework imports
producer and judge model calls within one evaluation flow
model identifiers propagated to producer and judge helpers
judge registration, scorer configuration, rubrics, and evaluator schemas
judge scores, verdicts, and evaluation result emission
distinct judges, human review, deterministic oracles, tests, and unresolved dynamic model IDs
